{"id":196,"date":"2026-06-25T09:45:57","date_gmt":"2026-06-25T09:45:57","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?post_type=trail&p=196"},"modified":"2026-07-14T10:02:08","modified_gmt":"2026-07-14T10:02:08","slug":"radar-trail","status":"publish","type":"trail","link":"https:\/\/beesayatv.test\/trails\/radar-trail\/","title":{"rendered":"Radar Trail | Sudlon 1 \/ Bagakay, Toledo | 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

Radar Trail is a point-to-point hike descending from Sudlon 1 toward Bagakay, Toledo. The route follows a mountain ridge through farmland, small mountain communities, and open countryside before ending at a bridge over the river that flows into Malubog Lake. The trail is generally easy to follow and suitable for hikers looking for a mostly downhill route with straightforward navigation.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
EASY<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>June 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>5.48 km (Point-to-Point)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>27 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>2-3 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 1 liter<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>90% trail, 10% road<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Supporter Access<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began near Sudlon 1 Barangay Hall, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was not available. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Easy<\/h3>
\u2713 Easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 No technical climbing required<\/div>
\u2713 Short hiking distance<\/div>
\u2713 Mostly downhill route<\/div>
\u2713 No river crossings, or crossings are simple<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> farmland<\/a> \u2022<\/span> ridge<\/a> \u2022<\/span> viewpoint<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route follows a mostly downhill mountain ridge through narrow trails, farmland, and small mountain communities. Several sections provide wide views of the surrounding countryside as the trail gradually descends toward Bagakay.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Although the route is generally easy to follow, some sections are uneven or rocky and may be hard on the knees during the descent. The hike concludes at a bridge crossing the river that flows into Malubog Lake.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy during rainy weather. The opening section was shaded, while the latter part of the route became more exposed. No stores were encountered along the trail.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was strong near the start before disappearing farther along the route. At least 1 liter of water was recommended. No other hikers were encountered during the hike, and insect activity was minimal.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The trail is well-trodden and regularly used by local residents and livestock. However, multiple side trails branch from the main route, and navigation can occasionally become confusing.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

In some areas, the trail may disappear where farmland overlaps the route. Following the GPX track or looking carefully for the continuation of the trail may be necessary.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

A trekking pole or walking stick is recommended because of the sustained downhill terrain. If the route is hiked in reverse, expect a continuous climb lasting several kilometers.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
